focal_character.name: Kwabena Asante

gate_visited.approach_taken: UNDETERMINED — focal character did not use a wrong approach in this gate; other entrants demonstrated the executor and information gatherer approaches

focal_character.ordinary_life_state: Kwabena sits at his desk in the Achimota dispatch office when Mr. Addo brings a thick folder of Tema route passenger manifests and asks him to find the lightest four-o'clock bus load to cancel.

problem_structure.unknown: what property the iron sorting mechanism actually sorts the crates by
data: dozens of wooden crates with painted numbers and weights
a heavy iron sorting mechanism with three wide angled chutes
a faint chalk line near the base of the mechanism
problem_structure.condition: the crates must be sorted by weight before the mechanism will turn

gate_details.correct_approach: Kwabena stopped at the entrance and looked at the structure of the room rather than its contents, named aloud what was wanted (the sequence), what was given (numbers and weights), and what bound them (the condition), then identified weight as the relevant property from the chalk line, and the gate closed.

new_texture: Kwabena's desk in the Achimota dispatch office is clear except for a single pen and a blank logbook.
A supervisor drops a thick manila folder of passenger manifests on the desk and asks for the lightest four-o'clock bus load to cancel.
Mr. Addo goes still and lowers his hand when Kwabena asks whether they are trying to cut a bus or find the empty seats.

anchor_update.observation: Grade 2. Information gatherer approach. Room gives complete data — every crate labelled, every label legible, every quantity recorded. Complete. Still. The catalogue is the mirror. Underlined: the catalogue answered every question the solver asked; the question the room was waiting for was never asked. Register 2 at minute 14. Register 3 at minute 18. Four minutes from sitting down to right question.
anchor_update.reader_can_suspect_update: The anchor is physically present inside the gates, observing and recording the exact mirror content and register shifts.
